title: Cactus v1：移动设备上的零延迟、全隐私跨平台大型语言模型推理
author: Sergio De Simone
content:
Cactus, a Y Combinator-backed startup, enables local AI inference to mobile phones, wearables, and other low-power devices through cross-platform, energy-efficient kernels and a native runtime. It delivers sub-50ms time-to-first-token for on-device inference, eliminates network latency, and defaults to complete privacy.

Version v1 of the SDK, now in beta, improves performance on lower-end hardware and adds optional cloud fallback to ensure greater reliability.

Cactus enables developers to deploy models locally in any app using its SDK, which offers native bindings for React Native, Flutter, and Kotlin Multiplatform. Native Swift support is still minimal and less mature as support for other languages, but iOS developers can use the Kotlin Multiplatform bindings within their Swift apps.

On iOS and Android devices, Cactus takes a more general approach to on-device AI inference than the platform-native solutions offered by Apple and Google, Apple Foundation frameworks and Google AI Edge, which are platform-specific and expose only a limited, vendor-controlled set of capabilities. Cactus supports a wide variety of models, including Qwen, Gemma, Llama, DeepSeek, Phi, Mistral and many others. For improved efficiency and performance, it supports a range of quantization levels, from FP32 down to 2-bit.

Cactus provides built-in model versioning and over-the-air updates. You can push new model versions to users without requiring app updates. The SDK handles downloading, caching, and switching between model versions seamlessly in the background

The Cactus SDK also supports fallback to cloud-based models for complex or large-context tasks, ensuring robustness and high availability. According to Cactus, "this addresses one of the most common requests from v0 users who needed guaranteed response times for critical user-facing features".

In version v1, Cactus has completely overhauled thitseir inference engine, transitioning from GGUF to a proprietary format and including optimized ARM-CPU kernels for better performance across all supported devices. The SDKs were rebuilt from the ground up to improve API consistency across languages, while maintaining backward compatibility wherever possible. The new version also introduces updated telemetry and monitoring systems.

[This] give[s] developers granular insights into their AI model performance, usage patterns, and potential optimization opportunities. This data-driven approach enables teams to make informed decisions about model selection and deployment strategies.

In addition to LLM inference, Cactus v1 also supports tool calling and voice transcription. The Flutter SDK additionally offers RAG fine-tuning, while the React Native SDK provides image embedding. According to Cactus’ roadmap, these features will soon be extended to other SDK variants, along with voice synthesis.

To better appreciate Cactus' performance across distinct devices, the company has published benchmarks using the LFM2-VL-450m and Whisper-Small models, measuring metrics such as tokens per second, prefill/decode, and more. The benchmarks employ INT8 quantization and leverage NPUs for real-time performance and large context handling. Results show that a Mac M4 Pro achieves 173 tok/s, an iPhone 17 Pro reaches 136 tok/s, a Galaxy S25 Ultra 91 tok/s, and a Raspberry Pi 5 24 tok/s.

For reference, the smallest model, gemma-3-270m-it, has a compressed footprint of 172 MB and supports only completion. Qwen3-0.6B occupies 394 MB and supports completion, tool calling, embedding, and speech. On the upper-end, Gemma-3-1b-it requires 642 MB, while Qwen3-1.7B takes 1,161 MB.

Cactus has also published chat apps for iOS and Android that can use distinct LLMs, allowing developers to benchmark latency and throughput when accessed through the Cactus SDK.

Cactus is open-source free for students, educators, non-profits, and small businesses. It can be cloned from GitHub.
aiSummary: Cactus v1 提供优化的引擎和软件开发工具包，实现在移动和低功耗设备上零延迟、全隐私且高性能的跨平台设备端大型语言模型推理。
oneSentenceSummary: Cactus v1 提供优化的引擎和软件开发工具包，实现在移动和低功耗设备上零延迟、全隐私且高性能的跨平台设备端大型语言模型推理。
summary: Cactus v1 是由 Y Combinator 支持的初创公司推出，为手机、可穿戴设备和其他低功耗设备上的设备端人工智能推理提供了解决方案，其特点是跨平台、节能内核和原生运行时。它实现了低于 50 毫秒的首次生成耗时，消除了网络延迟，并通过本地推理确保了完全隐私。测试版软件开发工具包提升了低端硬件上的性能，并包含可选的云端回退功能以提高可靠性。Cactus 支持广泛的大型语言模型，如通义千问、Gemma 和 Llama，以及各种量化级别，提供比平台原生解决方案更通用的方法。主要功能包括内置模型版本控制、无线更新（OTA 更新）、工具调用和语音转录。性能基准测试显示了在 iPhone 手机、Galaxy 手机和树莓派等不同设备上的每秒 Token 数表现出色。它还是开源的，对学生、教育工作者、非营利组织和小型企业免费，使其成为开发人员将人工智能集成到其应用程序中的强大且易于使用的工具。

mainPoints.point: Cactus v1 为移动和低功耗设备提供零延迟、私密、设备端大型语言模型推理，比平台原生解决方案提供更通用的方法。
mainPoints.explanation: 通过在本地运行大型语言模型，Cactus 消除了网络延迟，并通过设备端处理确保了数据隐私，提供了低于 50 毫秒的首次生成耗时。与苹果和谷歌的平台特定解决方案相比，这种方法更具普适性，为边缘设备提供了更广泛的模型支持和功能。

mainPoints.point: 该平台提供跨平台软件开发工具包，支持广泛的大型语言模型和量化级别，以及工具调用和语音转录等关键功能。
mainPoints.explanation: Cactus 为 React Native、Flutter 和 Kotlin Multiplatform 提供了原生绑定，支持各种模型（通义千问、Gemma、Llama 等）和量化（从 FP32 到 2 比特）。版本 1 还引入了工具调用和语音转录等关键功能，使其对开发人员而言具有高度灵活性和强大功能。

mainPoints.point: Cactus v1 包括优化的推理引擎、内置模型版本控制、OTA 更新和云端回退，增强了可靠性和开发人员洞察力。
mainPoints.explanation: 经过改进的引擎使用专有格式，并配备优化的 ARM-CPU 内核，以提升性能。模型版本控制、OTA 更新和可选的云端回退等功能增强了可靠性和开发人员控制。新的遥测和监控系统还为优化提供了精细的洞察。

keyQuotes: Cactus，这家由 Y Combinator 支持的初创公司，通过跨平台、节能内核和原生运行时，在手机、可穿戴设备和其他低功耗设备上实现了本地人工智能推理。
Cactus 提供内置模型版本控制和 OTA 更新。您可以将新的模型版本推送给用户，而无需强制应用更新。
Cactus 在设备端人工智能推理方面采取了比苹果和谷歌提供的平台原生解决方案更通用的方法。
Cactus v1 已彻底革新其推理引擎，从 GGUF 转向专有格式，并集成了优化的 ARM-CPU 内核，以提升所有支持设备上的性能。
